<content>1: import { ZodError } from 'zod'
2: import { ValidationError } from 'payload'
3: 
4: /**
5:  * Converts a Zod path array to dot notation
6:  * @example ["stem", 0, "value"] -> "stem.0.value"
7:  */
8: export function zodPathToDotPath(path: Array<string | number | symbol>): string {
9:   return path.map((segment) => String(segment)).join('.')
10: }
11: 
12: /**
13:  * Converts ZodError to Payload ValidationError format
14:  * @param zodError - The ZodError from schema validation
15:  * @param options - Configuration options
16:  * @param options.fieldPrefix - Prefix to add to all paths (e.g., "contentJson")
17:  * @param options.maxIssues - Maximum number of issues to include (default: 50)
18:  */
19: export function zodErrorToPayloadErrors(
20:   zodError: ZodError,
21:   options: {
22:     fieldPrefix: string
23:     maxIssues?: number
24:   },
25: ): Array<{ path: string; message: string }> {
26:   const { fieldPrefix, maxIssues = 50 } = options
27: 
28:   return zodError.issues.slice(0, maxIssues).map((issue) => ({
29:     path: issue.path.length > 0 ? `${fieldPrefix}.${zodPathToDotPath(issue.path)}` : fieldPrefix,
30:     message: issue.message,
31:   }))
32: }
33: 
34: /**
35:  * Throws a Payload ValidationError with properly formatted Zod errors
36:  * @param zodError - The ZodError from schema validation
37:  * @param fieldPrefix - The Payload field name (e.g., "contentJson", "answerSpecJson")
38:  */
39: export function throwPayloadValidationError(zodError: ZodError, fieldPrefix: string): never {
40:   const errors = zodErrorToPayloadErrors(zodError, { fieldPrefix })
41:   throw new ValidationError({ errors })
42: }
43: 
44: /**
45:  * Validates JSON content and throws Payload ValidationError on failure
46:  * @param fieldName - Name of the field for error messages
47:  * @param value - Value to validate (can be undefined/null)
48:  * @param schema - Zod schema to validate against
49:  */
50: export function validateJsonField<T>(
51:   fieldName: string,
52:   value: unknown,
53:   schema: { safeParse: (data: unknown) => { success: boolean; error?: ZodError; data?: T } },
54: ): T {
55:   // Handle missing value
56:   if (value === undefined || value === null) {
57:     throw new ValidationError({
58:       errors: [{ path: fieldName, message: 'Required' }],
59:     })
60:   }
61: 
62:   // Validate with Zod
63:   const result = schema.safeParse(value)
64: 
65:   if (!result.success && result.error) {
66:     throwPayloadValidationError(result.error, fieldName)
67:   }
68: 
69:   return result.data as T
70: }

72: async function restorePrevRunFiles(taskDir: string, _taskId: string): Promise<void> {

88:     try {

91:         timeout: 10000,
92:       })

95:       if (!fs.existsSync(prevRunDir)) {
96:         fs.mkdirSync(prevRunDir, { recursive: true })
97:       }
98: 
99:       // Write to prev-run/
100:       fs.writeFileSync(prevRunPath, gitShowOutput)
101:       logger.info(`  🔄 Restored ${file} from git to prev-run/`)
102: 
103:       // Also restore to main location if the main file doesn't exist
104:       if (!fs.existsSync(mainPath)) {

107:       }

545:   // Build spec stages based on profile
546:   const specOrder = ctx.profile === 'standard' ? SPEC_ORDER_STANDARD : SPEC_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT
547:   const filteredSpecOrder = ctx.input.clarify ? specOrder : specOrder.filter((s) => s !== 'clarify')

549:   // For spec_only pipelines, don't include impl stages — there's no plan.md to build from
550:   if (ctx.taskDef?.pipeline === 'spec_only') {
551:     return {
552:       stages: createStageDefinitions(ctx),
553:       order: [...filteredSpecOrder],

556: 
557:   // Build impl stages based on profile
558:   const implOrder = ctx.profile === 'standard' ? IMPL_ORDER_STANDARD : IMPL_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT
559: 
560:   // Combine: spec stages first (already completed), then impl stages (to run)
561:   return {

563:     order: [...filteredSpecOrder, ...implOrder],
564:   }

570: export function buildPipeline(
571:   mode: 'spec' | 'impl' | 'full' | 'rerun',
572:   profile: 'standard' | 'lightweight' | 'turbo',
573:   clarify: boolean,
574:   ctx: PipelineContext,
575: ): PipelineDefinition {
576:   const stages = createStageDefinitions(ctx)

578:   // Determine stage order based on mode and profile
579:   let order: PipelineStep[] = []

583:     const specOrder =
584:       profile === 'standard'

586:         : profile === 'turbo'
587:           ? SPEC_ORDER_TURBO
588:           : SPEC_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT
589:     // If clarify is disabled, remove it from the spec order
590:     const filteredSpecOrder = clarify ? specOrder : specOrder.filter((s) => s !== 'clarify')
591:     order = [...filteredSpecOrder]
592:   } else if (mode === 'impl') {
593:     // Implementation stages only
594:     const implOrder =
595:       profile === 'standard'
596:         ? IMPL_ORDER_STANDARD
597:         : profile === 'turbo'
598:           ? IMPL_ORDER_TURBO
599:           : IMPL_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT
600:     order = [...implOrder]
601:   } else if (mode === 'full' || mode === 'rerun') {
602:     // Full/rerun mode: include both spec and impl stages
603:     // This ensures the pipeline survives restarts — all stages are present
604:     // and the state machine efficiently skips completed ones
605:     const specOrder =
606:       profile === 'standard'
607:         ? SPEC_ORDER_STANDARD
608:         : profile === 'turbo'
609:           ? SPEC_ORDER_TURBO
610:           : SPEC_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT
611:     const implOrder =
612:       profile === 'standard'
613:         ? IMPL_ORDER_STANDARD
614:         : profile === 'turbo'
615:           ? IMPL_ORDER_TURBO
616:           : IMPL_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT
617:     const filteredSpecOrder = clarify ? specOrder : specOrder.filter((s) => s !== 'clarify')
618:     order = [...filteredSpecOrder, ...implOrder]
619:   }
620: 
621:   return { stages, order }
622: }

624: /**
625:  * Flatten pipeline order (including parallel stages) into a flat array of stage names
626:  */
627: export function flattenPipelineOrder(order: PipelineStep[]): StageName[] {
628:   const result: StageName[] = []
629:   for (const step of order) {
630:     if (typeof step === 'string') {
631:       result.push(step)
632:     } else if ('parallel' in step) {
633:       result.push(...step.parallel)
634:     }
635:   }

<content>1: /**
2:  * @fileType configuration
3:  * @domain cody | pipeline
4:  * @pattern stage-registry
5:  * @ai-summary Single source of truth for all pipeline stage metadata, types, and order arrays
6:  */
7: 
8: import ms from 'ms'
9: 
10: // ============================================================================
11: // Stage Names — the canonical list
12: // ============================================================================
13: 
14: /**
15:  * All valid stage names in the Cody pipeline.
16:  *
17:  * Excluded:
18:  * - 'spec' — merged into 'gap' months ago (ghost stage)
19:  * - 'autofix' — not a real pipeline stage; it's a sub-behavior of build feedback loops
20:  */
21: export const STAGE_NAMES = [
22:   'taskify',
23:   'gap',
24:   'clarify',
25:   'architect',
26:   'plan-gap',
27:   'test',
28:   'build',
29:   'commit',
30:   'review',
31:   'fix',
32:   'verify',
33:   'docs',
34:   'pr',
35: ] as const
36: 
37: export type StageName = (typeof STAGE_NAMES)[number]
38: 
39: /**
40:  * Named constant object for stage names — provides compile-time typo detection
41:  * and single-point rename capability. Use STAGES.BUILD instead of 'build' in new code.
42:  */
43: export const STAGES = {
44:   TASKIFY: 'taskify',
45:   GAP: 'gap',
46:   CLARIFY: 'clarify',
47:   ARCHITECT: 'architect',
48:   PLAN_GAP: 'plan-gap',
49:   TEST: 'test',
50:   BUILD: 'build',
51:   COMMIT: 'commit',
52:   REVIEW: 'review',
53:   FIX: 'fix',
54:   VERIFY: 'verify',
55:   DOCS: 'docs',
56:   PR: 'pr',
57: } as const satisfies Record<string, StageName>
58: 
59: // ============================================================================
60: // Stage Metadata
61: // ============================================================================
62: 
63: export interface StageMetadata {
64:   /** Expected output file name (e.g., 'task.json', 'plan.md') */
65:   outputFile: string
66:   /** Timeout in milliseconds */
67:   timeout: number
68:   /** Minimum complexity score to run (0 = always runs) */
69:   complexityThreshold: number
70:   /** Files the stage reads for context */
71:   contextFiles: string[]
72:   /** Handler dispatch type */
73:   type: 'agent' | 'scripted' | 'git' | 'gate'
74: }
75: 
76: /**
77:  * The stage registry — typed as Record<StageName, StageMetadata>.
78:  * Adding/removing from STAGE_NAMES without updating this record causes a compile error.
79:  */
80: export const STAGE_REGISTRY: Record<StageName, StageMetadata> = {
81:   taskify: {
82:     outputFile: 'task.json',
83:     timeout: ms('10m'),
84:     complexityThreshold: 0,
85:     contextFiles: ['task.md'],
86:     type: 'agent',
87:   },
88:   gap: {
89:     outputFile: 'gap.md',
90:     timeout: ms('15m'),
91:     complexityThreshold: 35,
92:     contextFiles: ['task.md', 'task.json'],
93:     type: 'agent',
94:   },
95:   clarify: {
96:     outputFile: 'questions.md',
97:     timeout: ms('10m'),
98:     complexityThreshold: 60,
99:     contextFiles: ['task.md', 'spec.md'],
100:     type: 'agent',
101:   },
102:   architect: {
103:     outputFile: 'plan.md',
104:     timeout: ms('30m'),
105:     complexityThreshold: 10,
106:     contextFiles: [
107:       'spec.md',
108:       'clarified.md',
109:       'rerun-feedback.md',
110:       'prev-run/plan.md',
111:       'prev-run/build.md',
112:       'prev-run/review.md',
113:     ],
114:     type: 'agent',
115:   },
116:   'plan-gap': {
117:     outputFile: 'plan-gap.md',
118:     timeout: ms('15m'),
119:     complexityThreshold: 50,
120:     contextFiles: ['spec.md', 'plan.md', 'task.json'],
121:     type: 'agent',
122:   },
123:   test: {
124:     outputFile: 'test.md',
125:     timeout: ms('20m'),
126:     complexityThreshold: 0,
127:     contextFiles: ['spec.md', 'clarified.md', 'plan.md', 'task.json'],
128:     type: 'agent',
129:   },
130:   build: {
131:     outputFile: 'build.md',
132:     timeout: ms('45m'),
133:     complexityThreshold: 0,
134:     contextFiles: [
135:       'spec.md',
136:       'clarified.md',
137:       'plan.md',
138:       'plan-gap.md',
139:       'context.md',
140:       'rerun-feedback.md',
141:       'build-errors.md',
142:       'review.md',
143:       'prev-run/build.md',
144:       'prev-run/review.md',
145:     ],
146:     type: 'agent',
147:   },
148:   commit: {
149:     outputFile: 'commit.md',
150:     timeout: ms('5m'),
151:     complexityThreshold: 0,
152:     contextFiles: ['task.json'],
153:     type: 'git',
154:   },
155:   review: {
156:     outputFile: 'review.md',
157:     timeout: ms('15m'),
158:     complexityThreshold: 30,
159:     contextFiles: ['review.md', 'build.md', 'plan.md', 'context.md', 'spec.md', 'clarified.md'],
160:     type: 'agent',
161:   },
162:   fix: {
163:     outputFile: 'fix.md',
164:     timeout: ms('45m'), // Increased from 30m — fixes often need more time than original build
165:     complexityThreshold: 0,
166:     contextFiles: [
167:       'verify-failures.md',
168:       'review.md',
169:       'rerun-feedback.md',
170:       'fix-summary.md',
171:       'build.md',
172:       'plan.md',
173:       'context.md',
174:       'spec.md',
175:       'clarified.md',
176:       'prev-run/build.md',
177:     ],
178:     type: 'agent',
179:   },
180:   verify: {
181:     outputFile: 'verify.md',
182:     timeout: ms('10m'),
183:     complexityThreshold: 0,
184:     contextFiles: [],
185:     type: 'scripted',
186:   },
187:   docs: {
188:     outputFile: 'docs.md',
189:     timeout: ms('10m'),
190:     complexityThreshold: 30,
191:     contextFiles: ['build.md', 'task.json', 'review.md', 'context.md'],
192:     type: 'agent',
193:   },
194:   pr: {
195:     outputFile: 'pr.md',
196:     timeout: ms('5m'),
197:     complexityThreshold: 0,
198:     contextFiles: [],
199:     type: 'git',
200:   },
201: }
202: 
203: // ============================================================================
204: // Helper Functions
205: // ============================================================================
206: 
207: /**
208:  * Get the output file name for a stage.
209:  * Falls back to `${stage}.md` for stages without a specific mapping.
210:  */
211: export function getStageOutputFile(stage: StageName): string {
212:   return STAGE_REGISTRY[stage].outputFile
213: }
214: 
215: /**
216:  * Get the timeout for a stage in milliseconds.
217:  */
218: export function getStageTimeout(stage: StageName): number {
219:   return STAGE_REGISTRY[stage].timeout
220: }
221: 
222: /**
223:  * Get the minimum complexity threshold for a stage.
224:  */
225: export function getStageComplexityThreshold(stage: StageName): number {
226:   return STAGE_REGISTRY[stage].complexityThreshold
227: }
228: 
229: /**
230:  * Get the context files a stage needs to read.
231:  */
232: export function getStageContextFiles(stage: StageName): string[] {
233:   return STAGE_REGISTRY[stage].contextFiles
234: }
235: 
236: /**
237:  * Runtime type guard — checks if a string is a valid StageName.
238:  */
239: export function isValidStageName(name: string): name is StageName {
240:   return (STAGE_NAMES as readonly string[]).includes(name)
241: }
242: 
243: /**
244:  * Assert that a string is a valid StageName, or throw.
245:  */
246: export function assertStageName(name: string): StageName {
247:   if (!isValidStageName(name)) {
248:     throw new Error(`Invalid stage name: '${name}'. Valid stages: ${STAGE_NAMES.join(', ')}`)
249:   }
250:   return name
251: }
252: 
253: // ============================================================================
254: // Resolve output file path on disk
255: // ============================================================================
256: 
257: /**
258:  * Build the full path to a stage's output file.
259:  * For stages not in the registry, falls back to `${stage}.md`.
260:  */
261: export function stageOutputFile(taskDir: string, stage: string): string {
262:   if (isValidStageName(stage)) {
263:     const filename = STAGE_REGISTRY[stage].outputFile
264:     return `${taskDir}/${filename}`
265:   }
266:   // Fallback for unknown stages (backward compat)
267:   return `${taskDir}/${stage}.md`
268: }
269: 
270: // ============================================================================
271: // Pipeline Order Arrays (typed)
272: // ============================================================================
273: 
274: export type TypedPipelineStep = StageName | { parallel: StageName[] }
275: 
276: export const SPEC_ORDER_STANDARD: StageName[] = ['taskify', 'gap', 'clarify']
277: export const SPEC_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT: StageName[] = ['taskify', 'clarify']
278: 
279: export const IMPL_ORDER_STANDARD: TypedPipelineStep[] = [
280:   'architect',
281:   'plan-gap',
282:   { parallel: ['test', 'build'] },
283:   'commit',
284:   'review',
285:   'fix',
286:   // NOTE: No second 'commit' here — fix stage commits via its post-action
287:   // (commit-task-files with tracked+task). A duplicate 'commit' entry would be
288:   // skipped by resolveNextStep since state.stages['commit'] is already completed.
289:   'verify',
290:   'pr',
291: ]
292: 
293: export const IMPL_ORDER_LIGHTWEIGHT: TypedPipelineStep[] = [
294:   'architect',
295:   { parallel: ['test', 'build'] },
296:   'commit',
297:   'review',
298:   'fix',
299:   'verify',
300:   'pr',
301: ]
302: 
303: /** Turbo spec order — minimal: just taskify (no gap/clarify) */
304: export const SPEC_ORDER_TURBO: StageName[] = ['taskify']
305: 
306: /** Turbo impl order — minimal: build→commit→verify→pr (no architect/review/fix) */
307: export const IMPL_ORDER_TURBO: TypedPipelineStep[] = ['build', 'commit', 'verify', 'pr']
308: 
309: /** Fix-only pipeline order for @cody fix mode */
310: export const FIX_ORDER: TypedPipelineStep[] = ['review', 'fix', 'verify', 'pr']
311: 
312: /** Full pipeline order for fix mode — runs the full impl pipeline with taskify prepended */
313: export const FIX_FULL_ORDER: TypedPipelineStep[] = [
314:   'taskify',
315:   'architect',
316:   'plan-gap',
317:   { parallel: ['test', 'build'] },
318:   'commit',
319:   'review',
320:   'fix',
321:   'verify',
322:   'pr',
323: ]
324: 
325: // ============================================================================
326: // Pipeline Utility Functions
327: // ============================================================================
328: 
329: /**
330:  * Flatten a typed pipeline step to its constituent stage names.
331:  */
332: export function flattenTypedStep(step: TypedPipelineStep): StageName[] {
333:   if (typeof step === 'string') return [step]
334:   return step.parallel
335: }
336: 
337: /**
338:  * Flatten an entire typed pipeline to a flat list of stage names.
339:  */
340: export function flattenTypedPipeline(steps: TypedPipelineStep[]): StageName[] {
341:   return steps.flatMap(flattenTypedStep)
342: }
343: 
344: /**
345:  * Spec-only stages (don't produce code).
346:  */
347: export const SPEC_STAGES: StageName[] = ['taskify', 'gap', 'clarify']
348: 
349: /**
350:  * Scripted stages that run directly without an LLM agent.
351:  */
352: export const SCRIPTED_STAGES: StageName[] = ['verify', 'commit', 'pr']

(End of file - total 352 lines)

import ms from 'ms'

/**

<content>1: /**
2:  * @fileType scenario
3:  * @domain cody | system-test
4:  * @ai-summary High-complexity full mode scenario - exercises ALL pipeline stages
5:  *
6:  * Steps:
7:  *   1. Create test version branch with cheap opencode.json
8:  *   2. Create GitHub issue
9:  *   3. Dispatch cody workflow (mode=full, complexity=65, version=branch)
10:  *   4. Poll for workflow completion
11:  *   5. Assert results (labels, PR, comments)
12:  *   6. Cleanup test version branch
13:  */
14: 
15: import { execFileSync } from 'child_process'
16: import * as fs from 'fs'
17: 
18: import { assertPRCreated, assertCommentExists } from '../lib'
19: import { CODY_WORKFLOW, SYSTEM_TEST_LABEL, ISSUE_TITLE_PREFIX } from '../lib/config'
20: import type { ScenarioContext, Scenario } from './types'
21: import type { ScenarioResult } from '../lib/report'
22: 
23: const ISSUE_BODY = `Create a new utility module at \`src/infra/utils/pipeline-health.ts\` that exports a \`PipelineHealthReport\` class for monitoring Cody pipeline health. The module should:
24: 
25: 1. Export a \`PipelineHealthReport\` class with methods: \`checkStageHealth(stage: string): HealthStatus\`, \`generateReport(): Report\`, and \`getRetryRecommendation(failedStage: string): RetryStrategy\`
26: 2. Define TypeScript interfaces: \`HealthStatus\`, \`Report\`, \`RetryStrategy\`
27: 3. Implement a \`getStageTimeout(stage: string): number\` helper that returns default timeouts per stage
28: 4. Add JSDoc comments on all exported members
29: 5. Include input validation using Zod schemas for all public method parameters
30: 6. Write a companion integration test at \`tests/unit/infra/utils/pipeline-health.test.ts\` covering all public methods
31: 
32: This is a medium-complexity feature that requires creating new TypeScript source files, defining types, implementing business logic, and writing tests.
33: 
34: **This is a SYSTEM TEST. The PR should NOT be merged.**`
35: 
36: // Branch name for test version - use timestamp to ensure uniqueness
37: const TEST_VERSION_BRANCH = (() => {
38:   const now = new Date()
39:   return `cody-test-${now.getFullYear()}${String(now.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')}${String(now.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')}`
40: })()
41: 
42: export const scenario02: Scenario = {
43:   name: '02-full-high-complexity',
44:   description:
45:     'High-complexity full mode - exercises ALL pipeline stages (taskify, gap, architect, plan-gap, build, commit, review, verify, pr)',
46:   timeoutMs: 90 * 60 * 1000, // 90 minutes
47: 
48:   async run(ctx: ScenarioContext): Promise<ScenarioResult> {
49:     const startTime = Date.now()
50:     const assertions: ScenarioResult['assertions'] = []
51: 
52:     let issueNumber: number | undefined = undefined
53:     let taskId: string | undefined
54:     let _workflowDispatchTime: string | undefined
55: 
56:     // Step 0: Create test version branch with opencode config
57:     // In replay mode: use mock config (cody uses recordings)
58:     // In record mode: use real config (cody uses real API, we capture calls separately)
59:     // Otherwise: use test (cheap) config
60:     const isReplayMode = process.env.MOCK_MODE === 'replay'
61:     const isRecordMode = process.env.MOCK_MODE === 'record'
62:     const configFile = isReplayMode ? 'opencode.mock.json' : 'opencode.test.json'
63:     const configLabel = isReplayMode ? 'mock' : 'cheap'
64: 
65:     ctx.log.info(`Creating test version branch: ${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH} with ${configLabel} config`)
66:     try {
67:       // Clean up any stale remote/local branch from previous runs
68:       try {
69:         execFileSync('git', ['push', 'origin', '--delete', TEST_VERSION_BRANCH], { stdio: 'pipe' })
70:         ctx.log.info(`Deleted stale remote branch: ${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH}`)
71:       } catch {
72:         // Branch doesn't exist remotely — that's fine
73:       }
74:       try {
75:         execFileSync('git', ['branch', '-D', TEST_VERSION_BRANCH], { stdio: 'pipe' })
76:         ctx.log.info(`Deleted stale local branch: ${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH}`)
77:       } catch {
78:         // Branch doesn't exist locally — that's fine
79:       }
80: 
81:       // Backup current opencode.json
82:       const currentOpencode = fs.readFileSync('./opencode.json', 'utf-8')
83: 
84:       // Replace with test or mock version
85:       fs.copyFileSync(`./${configFile}`, './opencode.json')
86: 
87:       // Create branch and push
88:       execFileSync('git', ['checkout', '-b', TEST_VERSION_BRANCH], { stdio: 'pipe' })
89:       execFileSync('git', ['add', 'opencode.json'], { stdio: 'pipe' })
90:       execFileSync(
91:         'git',
92:         ['commit', `-m`, `test: ${configLabel} models for system test`, '--no-verify'],
93:         {
94:           stdio: 'pipe',
95:         },
96:       )
97:       execFileSync('git', ['push', '-u', 'origin', TEST_VERSION_BRANCH], { stdio: 'pipe' })
98: 
99:       // Switch back to original branch
100:       execFileSync('git', ['checkout', '-'], { stdio: 'pipe' })
101: 
102:       // Restore original opencode.json
103:       fs.writeFileSync('./opencode.json', currentOpencode)
104: 
105:       ctx.log.info(`Pushed test version branch: ${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH}`)
106:       assertions.push({
107:         name: 'Test version branch created',
108:         passed: true,
109:         detail: TEST_VERSION_BRANCH,
110:       })
111:     } catch (error) {
112:       ctx.log.error({ error }, 'Failed to create test version branch')
113:       assertions.push({ name: 'Test version branch created', passed: false, detail: String(error) })
114:     }
115: 
116:     try {
117:       // Step 1: Create issue
118:       ctx.log.info('Creating issue...')
119:       const title = `${ISSUE_TITLE_PREFIX} Add pipeline health monitoring utility module`
120:       issueNumber = ctx.gh.createIssue(title, ISSUE_BODY, [SYSTEM_TEST_LABEL]) ?? undefined
121: 
122:       if (!issueNumber) {
123:         throw new Error('Failed to create issue')
124:       }
125:       assertions.push({ name: 'Issue created', passed: true, detail: `#${issueNumber}` })
126:       ctx.log.info(`Created issue #${issueNumber}`)
127: 
128:       // Step 2: Dispatch pipeline with complexity override
129:       const now = new Date()
130:       const yy = String(now.getFullYear()).slice(-2)
131:       const mm = String(now.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')
132:       const dd = String(now.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')
133:       taskId = `${yy}${mm}${dd}-systest-${ctx.runId}`
134:       _workflowDispatchTime = now.toISOString()
135: 
136:       ctx.log.info(
137:         `Dispatching pipeline: task=${taskId}, complexity=65, version=${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH}`,
138:       )
139: 
140:       execFileSync(
141:         'gh',
142:         [
143:           'workflow',
144:           'run',
145:           CODY_WORKFLOW,
146:           '-f',
147:           `task_id=${taskId}`,
148:           '-f',
149:           'mode=full',
150:           '-f',
151:           `issue_number=${issueNumber}`,
152:           '-f',
153:           'complexity=65',
154:           '-f',
155:           'clarify=false',
156:           '-f',
157:           'dry_run=false',
158:           '-f',
159:           `version=${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH}`,
160:           '-f',
161:           `use_mock=${isReplayMode}`,
162:           '--repo',
163:           ctx.repo,
164:         ],
165:         { env: { ...process.env }, stdio: 'pipe' },
166:       )
167: 
168:       assertions.push({ name: 'Pipeline dispatched', passed: true, detail: taskId })
169:       ctx.log.info(`Dispatched pipeline for task ${taskId}`)
170: 
171:       // Step 3: Poll for pipeline completion via issue labels.
172:       // The cody pipeline can span multiple workflow runs (initial dispatch,
173:       // gate approval reruns, pipeline-fixer retries), so polling for a single
174:       // workflow run is unreliable. Instead, poll the issue labels for the
175:       // terminal state: cody:done or cody:failed.
176:       ctx.log.info('Polling for pipeline completion via issue labels (up to 90 min)...')
177:       const terminalLabels = ['cody:done', 'cody:failed']
178:       const pollStart = Date.now()
179:       const maxWaitMs = 90 * 60 * 1000
180:       const pollIntervalMs = 30 * 1000
181:       let finalLabel: string | undefined
182:       let gateApproved = false
183: 
184:       while (Date.now() - pollStart < maxWaitMs) {
185:         const labelsOutput = execFileSync(
186:           'gh',
187:           [
188:             'issue',
189:             'view',
190:             String(issueNumber),
191:             '--repo',
192:             ctx.repo,
193:             '--json',
194:             'labels',
195:             '--jq',
196:             '[.labels[].name]',
197:           ],
198:           { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' },
199:         ).trim()
200:         const labels: string[] = labelsOutput ? JSON.parse(labelsOutput) : []
201:         const terminal = labels.find((l: string) => terminalLabels.includes(l))
202:         if (terminal) {
203:           finalLabel = terminal
204:           break
205:         }
206:         // Auto-approve risk gate when detected
207:         if (labels.includes('risk-gated') && !gateApproved) {
208:           ctx.log.info('  Risk gate detected — posting @cody approve...')
209:           try {
210:             execFileSync(
211:               'gh',
212:               [
213:                 'issue',
214:                 'comment',
215:                 String(issueNumber),
216:                 '--repo',
217:                 ctx.repo,
218:                 '--body',
219:                 '@cody approve',
220:               ],
221:               { env: { ...process.env }, stdio: 'pipe' },
222:             )
223:             gateApproved = true
224:             ctx.log.info('  Auto-approved risk gate')
225:           } catch (error) {
226:             ctx.log.warn({ error }, 'Failed to auto-approve risk gate')
227:           }
228:         }
229:         ctx.log.info(`  Labels: [${labels.join(', ')}] — waiting...`)
230:         await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, pollIntervalMs))
231:       }
232: 
233:       if (!finalLabel) {
234:         throw new Error(`Pipeline did not reach terminal state within ${maxWaitMs}ms`)
235:       }
236: 
237:       assertions.push({
238:         name: 'Pipeline completed',
239:         passed: true,
240:         detail: `Terminal label: ${finalLabel}`,
241:       })
242: 
243:       // Step 4: Assert results
244:       if (finalLabel === 'cody:done') {
245:         assertions.push({ name: 'Pipeline succeeded (cody:done)', passed: true })
246:       } else {
247:         assertions.push({
248:           name: 'Pipeline succeeded (cody:done)',
249:           passed: false,
250:           detail: `Expected cody:done, got: ${finalLabel}`,
251:         })
252:       }
253: 
254:       // Check task comment
255:       try {
256:         assertCommentExists(ctx.gh, issueNumber, /Task created:/)
257:         assertions.push({ name: 'Task marker comment', passed: true })
258:       } catch (error) {
259:         assertions.push({ name: 'Task marker comment', passed: false, detail: String(error) })
260:       }
261: 
262:       // Check PR created — match by issue number since the branch name
263:       // contains the issue number (e.g. feat/260319-systest-934-...), not the run ID
264:       try {
265:         const pr = assertPRCreated(ctx.repo, new RegExp(String(issueNumber)))
266:         assertions.push({
267:           name: 'PR created',
268:           passed: true,
269:           detail: `PR #${pr.number}: ${pr.branch}`,
270:         })
271:       } catch (error) {
272:         assertions.push({ name: 'PR created', passed: false, detail: String(error) })
273:       }
274: 
275:       // Cleanup: delete test version branch
276:       ctx.log.info(`Cleaning up test version branch: ${TEST_VERSION_BRANCH}`)
277:       try {
